title: Prepare and publish the next immutable release
outOfScope: Changing release workflow security controls or publishing a release when any required gate fails.
Claiming enterprise certification, runtime isolation, trusted identity, or provider evidence before the workflow produces it.
Cleaning execution-plan documents; that is the final Work Item.

sources.reason: Published release contract and current version identity.
Canonical candidate/published state and source identity.
Immutable source, tag, asset, smoke, and compatibility authority.
Release evidence and NO-GO boundary documentation.

riskAssessment.reason: Release preparation is metadata-bounded; final publication remains fail-closed behind the existing workflow and exact SHA evidence.

executionDecision.reason: User explicitly authorized release publication after Work Items 1–15 completed their full lifecycles.

preReviewWarnings: Verify SOURCE_COMMIT equals the freshly resolved default branch commit and no tag exists before dispatch.
Do not report publication until the workflow, release asset, SBOM, provenance, digest, smoke, and compatibility evidence succeeds.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Published metadata advances to v0.5.34 only from the latest verified default-branch source commit, with v0.5.35 retained as the next unpublished candidate.
Release workflow evidence binds SOURCE_COMMIT, default branch, immutable tag, workflow run, release assets, SBOM, provenance, and evidence bundle digest.
Exact-SHA smoke and compatibility evidence pass before publication; any failure leaves publication unchanged.
Release notes and distribution documentation preserve enterprise-level NO-GO boundaries.
guidelines: Preserve release source identity evidence and immutable tag rules.
Do not claim publication success without provider workflow and asset evidence.
Keep candidate, published, and provider-evidence states distinct.

intent.problem: The completed remediation must be published as a new immutable release, while current candidate metadata and release-state source identity must be refreshed to the latest main commit without treating pending provider evidence as published fact.
constraints: Use the existing release workflow as final evidence authority and do not bypass its exact-SHA, tag, smoke, compatibility, asset, SBOM, provenance, or digest gates.
Keep release preparation and publication state truthful; failure must leave the prior public release unchanged.
Do not alter enterprise boundary claims or the final execution-plan cleanup scope.
intent.rationale: A source-bound release preparation plus workflow publication makes the completed remediation consumable while preserving fail-closed release evidence.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly authorized continuous execution of the review remediation plan, including publishing the new version and its full PR lifecycle.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Candidate and published metadata remain distinct and internally consistent
Exact source commit, default branch, tag, and workflow run are linked
Release assets, SBOM, provenance, and evidence digest are required before publication
Release notes preserve enterprise NO-GO boundaries

rawUserRequest: 发布新版本；仅在全部整改工单完成后发布，失败不发布。

declaredIntent.summary: Prepare and publish the next immutable release from the latest default branch with exact source-bound provider evidence.
